window_summary: This window tracks the recent jesseduffield/lazydocker release stream from early versions through the latest updates, highlighting key feature additions, bug fixes, and configuration changes.

chunks.title: v0.17.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.17.1
Tag: v0.17.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-05-09T12:31:41Z; created_at: 2022-05-09T12:27:29Z
Release notes:
They say a picture is worth a thousand words. Here's two thousand words for your viewing pleasure:
Before:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8456633/167385086-1db58e61-29c4-441d-a7f2-8c8c95338bad.png)
After:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8456633/167385465-f4db1a96-2907-4828-8662-7bb35fa3bd96.png)
## Changes:
* CPU usage should now be under control (idles at 1-2% CPU on my machine compared to 100% before). There's more room for improvement but this is a great start.
* There's a new tab in the main view for viewing a container's environment variables (thanks @glendsoza)
* Containers are now sorted by status (you can restore the old sorting logic with `gui.legacySortContainers: true` in your config (thanks @lpessoa)
* No more anonymous reporting popup because we stopped doing telemetry ages ago
## Contributor changes
* We're now on go 1.18 meaning generics are a go
* we're now using github actions rather than circle CI
## Note from maintainer
Since the dawn of this app, crazy CPU usage has been an embarrassing issue. By happenstance my friend @ed-bassett told me 'Jesse you really need to fix Lazydocker's CPU issues. Did you know you can use Docker's event stream to avoid polling for changes?' Long story short that was a much easier change that I expected, and I can't believe it took me so long to get around to it!
If anybody's been paying attention to Lazydocker's sibling repo Lazygit they'll know that I spend far more of my time over there, meaning things move slower over here. With that said, I'm trying to find ways of dedicating more time to both repos. If you want to support my open source work please consider sponsoring me via the sponsor button at the top of this page. Every bit helps! Thanks to all who have supported me so far.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release v0.17.1, the release notes highlight that CPU usage is now under control, idling at 1-2% instead of 100% before. A new tab in the main view shows a container's environment variables. Containers are now sorted by status, with an option to restore legacy sorting via 'gui.legacySortContainers: true'. The anonymous reporting popup is removed since telemetry was stopped. Contributor changes include moving to Go 1.18 and using GitHub Actions instead of CircleCI. The maintainer notes that the CPU fix was achieved by using Docker's event stream to avoid polling.

chunks.title: v0.18
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.18
Tag: v0.18; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-05-11T11:00:01Z; created_at: 2022-05-11T10:37:15Z
Release notes:
## v0.18 Changes
* If you didn't see the release notes from v0.17.1, know that CPU usage is now well under control. If you ever experience high CPU while using lazydocker, please raise an issue.
* When docker is not running, we now wait for it to start up instead of crashing
* fixed issue where container CPU was reported lower than expected (sometimes at zero percent)
* Less reliance on docker binary (though we're not quite ready to do away with the binary entirely). This makes for noticeably faster loading of container logs
* Updated rendering libary (gocui) includes various bug fixes, supports more terminals (e.g. alacritty), and notably introduces scrollbars! (that you cannot interact with that but are nice regardless)
* We are now sorting images by name for easier browsing
* You can now force remove images (with an admittedly clunky UI for now)
* You can now start (as opposed to restart) a service with `shift+S`
* Fixed bug where toggling the hiding of stopped containers did not immediately apply
* version info is now built in upon doing `go install` meaning fewer users should encountered the 'unversioned' version at the bottom left of the screen (instead they'll see the git commit hash)
* Fixed the Dockerfile to support go 1.18
* Performance improvements
## Changed defaults
We now wrap content in the main panel by default. You can restore the old behaviour with:
```yaml
gui:
wrapMainPanel: false
```
Timestamps in container logs are no longer shown by default. You can restore them with:
```yaml
logs:
timestamps: false
```
Now that we're using the docker SDK (rather than binary) for viewing container logs, the `commandTemplates.containerLogs` is now longer recognised. Instead you can configure logging like so:
```yaml
logs:
timestamps: false
since: '60m'
```
A notable exception to this is that if you press 'm' to view logs on a container we'll still use the binary. That'll also be switched to using the SDK (meaning the above config options will apply to that too) in a future relase.
## Note from maintainer
I've closed off quite a few issues as having been resolved, and the issues that remain are surprisingly do-able. Many wanted features like searching in the main panel and resizing panels are already solved problems in lazygit and can be easily ported over.
So, if you're looking for some open source work, or just want to see your desired feature in lazydocker, consider picking up an issue. I'll be happy to give pointers.
As always, thanks to the contributors who help out in the repo and thanks to all my sponsors. If you'd like to support lazydocker's development, please consider sponsoring me by clicking the sponsor button at the top of this page. Every bit helps!
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release v0.18, the release notes state that CPU usage is well under control. When Docker is not running, the application now waits for it to start instead of crashing. A bug where container CPU was reported lower than expected is fixed. There is less reliance on the Docker binary, resulting in faster loading of container logs. The updated rendering library (gocui) includes bug fixes, supports more terminals like Alacritty, and introduces scrollbars. Images are now sorted by name, and force removal of images is possible. Services can be started with 'shift+S'. A bug where toggling the hiding of stopped containers did not immediately apply is fixed. Version info is now built in upon 'go install'. The Dockerfile is fixed to support Go 1.18. Performance improvements are included. Defaults are changed: content in the main panel is now wrapped by default, timestamps in container logs are no longer shown by default, and the 'commandTemplates.containerLogs' is no longer recognized; instead, logging can be configured with 'logs.timestamps' and 'logs.since'.

chunks.title: v0.19.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.19.0
Tag: v0.19.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-10-13T01:14:23Z; created_at: 2022-10-10T20:51:42Z
Release notes:
## Changelog
### Features:
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/372 ports are displayed in the containers and services panels
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/317 (@mark2185 ) support for `docker pause` command via 'p' key
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/346 ability to start shell from services panel via 'E' key
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/372 the current window can be expanded to take up half the screen or the full screen using the '+' and '_' keys (like in lazygit)
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/366 (@miles170 ) string replacements for image names. For example:
```yml
replacements:
imageNamePrefixes:
'123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com': '<prod>'
'923456789999.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com': '<dev>'
```
This prevents  the image name column from being ridiculously long.
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/372 use blue background colour on selected line for better visibility
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/367 (@blennster ) support for tab and backtab navigation
*  https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/342 view container (in stdout) logs via SDK rather than via docker binary
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/375 improve sorting of volumes to show named volumes at the top
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/352 (@ rmtsrc ) image name included in config tab
### Bug fixes:
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/372 fixed some focus-related issues
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/362 (@tilliwilli ) fixes bug where text was white in light themes
### Maintenance:
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/358 (@Syriiin ) updated FAQ to include steps for docker compose V2 usage
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/373 (@gusandrioli ) keybinding cheatsheets are now marked as auto-generated to reduce confusion when people want to update the cheatsheets.
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/355 (@DasFaultier ) improved install script
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/356 extracted process kill logic for use in lazygit
* https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/368 fixed linting errors
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release v0.19.0, the release notes include features such as displaying ports in the containers and services panels, support for the 'docker pause' command via the 'p' key, ability to start a shell from the services panel via the 'E' key, expanding the current window to half or full screen using '+' and '_' keys, string replacements for image names to shorten long image name columns, using a blue background color on the selected line for better visibility, support for tab and backtab navigation, viewing container logs via SDK rather than the Docker binary, improving sorting of volumes to show named volumes at the top, and including the image name in the config tab. Bug fixes address focus-related issues and a bug where text was white in light themes. Maintenance updates include FAQ steps for Docker Compose V2, marking keybinding cheatsheets as auto-generated, improving the install script, extracting process kill logic for use in lazygit, and fixing linting errors.

chunks.title: v0.21.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.21.0
Tag: v0.21.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-07-21T09:19:13Z; created_at: 2023-07-21T09:14:23Z
Release notes:
Lots of good stuff in this release. Special shoutout to @Tony-Sol for adding the networks view!
## What's Changed
* Add networks view by @Tony-Sol in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/424
* Allow configuring number of logs to tail by @jesseduffield in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/405
* Wrap custom commands on shell by @gusandrioli in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/387
* Use service name as service ID by @nakaakist in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/407
* Allow setting default screenMode by @Rdkang in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/421
* Add selectedLineBgColor theme customization by @tiagovla in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/420
* Allow showing compact container statuses by @martini1992 in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/419
* Render container stats and configs as yaml by @Tony-Sol in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/444
* Display hotkeys to detach from the container by @glendsoza in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/411
* Chinese translation by @tabris233 in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/441
* add dev-container by @jesseduffield in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/401
* os: fix linter issue by @dawidd6 in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/442
* Docs: explain go templates in custom commands by @jchvz in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/428
* Bump github.com/gogo/protobuf from 1.3.1 to 1.3.2 by @dependabot in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/432
* Bump github.com/opencontainers/image-spec from 1.0.1 to 1.0.2 by @dependabot in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/439
* Bump github.com/docker/distribution from 2.8.0+incompatible to 2.8.2+incompatible by @dependabot in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/448
* Bump github.com/docker/distribution from 2.7.1+incompatible to 2.8.0+incompatible by @dependabot in
## New Contributors
* @nakaakist made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/407
* @Tony-Sol made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/424
* @dependabot made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/432

chunks.title: v0.23.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.23.0
Tag: v0.23.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-10-11T10:59:20Z; created_at: 2023-10-10T22:18:14Z
Release notes:
The important part of this release is that we now honour the host specified in the current docker context (courtesy of @rajiv-k !).
But thanks to all contributors who helped with this release!
Apologies for taking so long to cut new releases with lazydocker. I really do want to spend more time on this project but lazygit consumes pretty much all of my free time. I've got a [PR](https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/406) in the works for changing things like timestamps/wrap/autoscroll/etc when viewing logs but it's in limbo until I find the time to get back to it.
## Changelog
### Features
* d2fa5e9 feat(docker): Honour the host specified in current docker context (#464) (@rajiv-k )
* 966570c Forward env vars to commands (@jesseduffield )
### Translations
* 50582db feat(i18n): 🌐 Portuguese translation (#479) (@miguelfferraz  )
* d2dd4db i18n: spanish translation (#473) (@luisnquin )
* 722fb88 Fix mistakes in French translation (#427) (@paulus85 )
* 42a9668 Fix polish translation (#467) (@panproteus )
### Maintenance
* 9e90d2f Mention JSON schema (#474) (@EmilyGraceSeville7cf )
* e69449c Use go 1.20 (@jesseduffield )
* 953e560 fix: Maintenance typo (#477) (@testwill )
* 39e694f Add timeout to go linter (@jesseduffield )
* 2dda39d Appease linter (@jesseduffield )
* 6473810 Bump docker (@jesseduffield )
* 71e8e43 Bump version tag in installation docs for asdf (#476) (@helgeblod )
* f25a5eb Bump x/net (@jesseduffield )
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release v0.23.0, the release notes honor the host specified in the current Docker context, forward environment variables to commands, add Portuguese and Spanish translations, fix mistakes in French and Polish translations, mention JSON schema, use Go 1.20, fix a maintenance typo, add a timeout to the Go linter, appease the linter, bump Docker and x/net, and bump the version tag in installation docs for asdf.

chunks.title: v0.23.3
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.23.3
Tag: v0.23.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-05-26T04:23:24Z; created_at: 2024-05-26T04:19:55Z
Release notes:
Some small changes in this release:
* Fixed a bug where logs did not appear in the main view as expected, especially on startup (cannot believe how long this was a thing for)
* Fixed bug where logs did not render correctly after returning from a subprocess (again; I just can't believe it)
* Fixed portugese typo (thanks Vininhos)
* Updated docs to use correct go version (thanks João Thallis)
* Added config for border style (thanks Scott McKendry)
* Updated appearance of scrollbars to be less ugly
Shameless plug: check out my new product https://www.subble.com which helps your company manage its SaaS subscriptions (discovery, licence management, etc)
Slightly less shameless plug: If you find value in my open source work, please sponsor me at https://github.com/sponsors/jesseduffield. Every donation helps!
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release v0.23.3, the release notes fix a bug where logs did not appear in the main view as expected, especially on startup, fix a bug where logs did not render correctly after returning from a subprocess, fix a Portuguese typo, update docs to use the correct Go version, add config for border style, and update the appearance of scrollbars to be less ugly.

chunks.title: v0.24.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: jesseduffield/lazydocker
Release: v0.24.1
Tag: v0.24.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-11-23T06:33:25Z; created_at: 2024-11-23T06:24:22Z
Release notes:
Thanks to everybody who contributed in this release!
## What's Changed
* fix: Properly support remote docker daemon over ssh by @rajiv-k in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/511
* fix lazydocker breaking when having default context in config by @peauc in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/552
* Bump lots of dependencies for various improvements by @thaJeztah in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/561
- This fixed a bug I had where sometimes lazydocker didn't realise it was in a folder with a docker compose file.
* Add hint to what .DockerCompose is in documentation by @hauskens in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/554
* Use 'docker compose' by default by @jesseduffield in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/563
* go.mod: github.com/docker/docker, docker/cli v27.1.1 by @thaJeztah in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/568
## New Contributors
* @github-actions made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/536
* @peauc made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/552
* @thaJeztah made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/561
* @hauskens made their first contribution in https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/pull/554
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/compare/v0.23.3...v0.24.1
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release v0.24.1, the release notes properly support remote Docker daemon over SSH, fix breaking when having default context in config, bump many dependencies which fixed a bug where lazydocker didn't realize it was in a folder with a docker compose file, add a hint to what .DockerCompose is in documentation, use 'docker compose' by default, and update go.mod dependencies.

questions.query_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker releases, which release introduces a networks view and allows configuring the number of logs to tail?

answer_support.answer_text: add a networks view, allow configuring the number of logs to tail

questions.query_text: Across the jesseduffield/lazydocker releases, how does the CPU usage improvement described in v0.17.1 differ from the CPU-related change in v0.8?

options.text: v0.17.1 reduces CPU usage by using Docker's event stream to avoid polling, while v0.8 reduces CPU usage caused by tickers.
v0.17.1 removes all tickers, while v0.8 adds more tickers for better monitoring.
Both releases achieve CPU reduction by removing the Docker binary dependency.
v0.17.1 focuses on memory optimization, while v0.8 focuses on disk I/O reduction.

questions.expected_answer: v0.17.1 reduces CPU usage by using Docker's event stream to avoid polling, while v0.8 reduces CPU usage caused by tickers.

answer_support.answer_text: CPU usage is now under control ... using Docker's event stream to avoid polling for changes
reduce cpu usage caused by tickers

questions.query_text: In the jesseduffield/lazydocker release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds a health check to the container list and corrects Dutch typos, (2) a release that adds a networks view and allows configuring the number of logs to tail, (3) a release that properly supports remote Docker daemon over SSH and uses 'docker compose' by default, (4) a release that fixes a panic by validating Names slice length and upgrades Go to 1.23.

answer_support.answer_text: add a health check to the container list ... Corrected some typos dutch
Add networks view ... Allow configuring number of logs to tail
fix: Properly support remote docker daemon over ssh ... Use 'docker compose' by default
Fix panic: validate Names slice length ... Upgrade Go version from 1.21 to 1.23
